title: selliguea treubii
intro: Selliguea treubii, also known as the Malaysian polypody, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Selliguea treubii include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the leaves for use in capsules or tablets.
